data.title: CD Projekt S.A. Reports Strong Q3 2025 Earnings
data.description:
Cd Projekt S.A. ( (OTGLY) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Cd Projekt S.A. presented to its investors.

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for the data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.

CD Projekt S.A. is a prominent video game developer and publisher based in Poland, known for its critically acclaimed titles such as The Witcher series and Cyberpunk 2077, operating primarily in the video game industry.

In its latest earnings report for the third quarter of 2025, CD Projekt S.A. demonstrated robust financial performance, driven by the continued success of its flagship games and strategic expansions. The company reported significant growth in net sales and profitability compared to the previous year.

Key financial metrics revealed a notable increase in net sales, reaching PLN 792,112 thousand, up from PLN 652,140 thousand in the same period last year. Operating profit also saw a substantial rise to PLN 362,675 thousand, while net profit attributable to shareholders increased to PLN 348,430 thousand. This growth was largely fueled by the strong performance of Cyberpunk 2077, including its Phantom Liberty expansion, and the release of the Cyberpunk 2077 Ultimate Edition for Nintendo Switch 2.

Additionally, CD Projekt S.A. maintained a healthy balance sheet with total assets amounting to PLN 3,299,084 thousand and equity of PLN 3,061,479 thousand. The company continued to invest in future projects, with significant expenditure on development projects such as The Witcher 4 and Cyberpunk 2.

Looking ahead, CD Projekt S.A. remains focused on expanding its portfolio and enhancing its market position through ongoing development of new titles and leveraging its existing franchises. The management’s outlook suggests continued growth and innovation in the coming quarters, supported by strategic investments and a strong financial foundation.

Alarum Technologies Ltd., a global provider of web data collection solutions, is making strides in the AI training-data market with its advanced hybrid proxy network. The company recently reported a record third-quarter revenue growth of 81% year-over-year, reaching $13 million, driven by increased demand from AI model developers. Despite a net profit of $0.1 million, Alarum is focusing on long-term market-share capture, investing heavily in infrastructure to support the burgeoning AI sector.

The company’s strategic decision to prioritize market leadership over immediate profitability is evident in its financial performance. Key metrics include a 26% increase in paying customers and a 17% rise in average revenue per customer. Alarum’s AI-centric products, such as DataSets and Website Unblocker, are contributing significantly to revenue growth, with triple-digit increases in customer adoption.

Alarum’s management remains optimistic about the company’s future, anticipating continued revenue growth and margin expansion as its infrastructure investments begin to pay off. The company expects fourth-quarter revenues to maintain a high level, projecting approximately $12 million, a 63% increase year-over-year.

Looking ahead, Alarum is well-positioned to capitalize on the explosive growth of the AI training-data market. As the company continues to expand its capacity and refine its offerings, it aims to solidify its role as a critical infrastructure provider for AI labs worldwide, setting the stage for accelerated revenue and profitability in the coming years.

Lee Enterprises is a digital-first subscription platform that provides local news and advertising services across 72 markets in the United States. The company is known for its focus on digital transformation and its significant presence in the media industry.

In its latest earnings report, Lee Enterprises highlighted a growth in digital subscription revenue and an increase in Adjusted EBITDA on a comparable basis. The company continues to focus on its Three Pillar Digital Growth Strategy, which has resulted in sustained performance in the digital sector.

Key financial metrics from the fourth quarter of fiscal 2025 include a total operating revenue of $139 million, with digital revenue comprising 53% of this figure. Digital-only subscription revenue saw a year-over-year increase of 16%, while digital advertising and marketing services accounted for 74% of total advertising revenue. Despite a net loss of $6 million, the company reported an Adjusted EBITDA of $15 million, reflecting a $2 million growth on a comparable basis.

For the full fiscal year, Lee Enterprises reported a total operating revenue of $562 million, with digital revenue remaining stable compared to the previous year. The company continues to manage its expenses effectively, with operating expenses decreasing by 7% year-over-year. The strategic termination of its pension plan is expected to enhance balance sheet flexibility.

Looking ahead, Lee Enterprises anticipates continued growth in Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2026, driven by its digital transformation efforts and cost management strategies. The company remains focused on capturing long-term value and leading the next phase of its digital evolution.

data.title: Ingles Markets Reports Resilient 2025 Financial Results
data.description:
Ingles Markets Inc ( (IMKTA) ) has released its Q4 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Ingles Markets Inc presented to its investors.

TipRanks Black Friday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for the data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Ingles Markets, Incorporated, a leading grocery retailer operating in six southeastern states, is headquartered in Asheville, North Carolina, and manages 197 supermarkets alongside neighborhood shopping centers and a fluid dairy facility.

Ingles Markets reported its financial results for the fourth quarter and fiscal year 2025, highlighting a year of strategic improvements and resilience despite challenges. The company emphasized its commitment to enhancing customer experience and acknowledged the hard work of its associates throughout the year.

For the fourth quarter of 2025, Ingles Markets achieved net sales of $1.37 billion, a slight decrease from the previous year’s $1.40 billion. However, the company saw a significant improvement in gross profit, which rose to $334.7 million, or 24.5% of sales, compared to 21.4% in the prior year. Notably, the company turned around its net income to $25.7 million from a net loss of $1.5 million in the same quarter of 2024, which had been impacted by hurricane-related impairments.

On an annual basis, Ingles Markets reported net sales of $5.33 billion for fiscal year 2025, down from $5.64 billion in 2024. Despite the decrease in sales, the company maintained a stable gross profit margin of 23.9%. The fiscal year net income was $83.6 million, a decline from $105.5 million in 2024. The company also reduced its capital expenditures significantly from $210.9 million in 2024 to $114.5 million in 2025, indicating a more conservative investment approach.

Looking ahead, Ingles Markets remains optimistic about its financial resources and operational strategies. The company believes it has sufficient financial resources to meet its capital expenditures, debt service, and working capital requirements, positioning it well for future growth and stability.

Cheetah Mobile is a China-based IT company specializing in AI innovation, offering a range of internet products and services on PCs and mobile devices, and providing AI service robots and robotic arms to international clients. In its third quarter of 2025, Cheetah Mobile reported a significant financial turnaround, achieving its first quarterly operating profit in six years with a 49.6% year-over-year revenue increase, primarily driven by a 150.8% surge in its AI and others segment. data.title: Pharvaris Reports Q3 2025 Financial Results and Updates
data.description:
Pharvaris N.V. ( (PHVS) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Pharvaris N.V. presented to its investors.

TipRanks Black Friday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for the data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Pharvaris N.V. is a late-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on developing novel, oral bradykinin B2 receptor antagonists to address unmet needs in bradykinin-mediated diseases such as hereditary angioedema (HAE) and acquired angioedema due to C1 inhibitor deficiency (AAE-C1INH).

In its third quarter 2025 financial results, Pharvaris highlighted advancements in its clinical development programs, including the anticipated release of topline data from its pivotal Phase 3 RAPIDe-3 study for the on-demand treatment of HAE attacks by the end of 2025. The company also reported progress in the CHAPTER-3 study for prophylactic treatment, and the initiation of the CREAATE study for both prophylactic and on-demand treatment of AAE-C1INH attacks.

Key financial metrics from the third quarter reveal a strong liquidity position with cash and cash equivalents totaling €329 million as of September 30, 2025. Research and development expenses increased to €29.8 million, reflecting ongoing investment in clinical trials, while general and administrative expenses decreased to €9.8 million. The company reported a reduced quarterly loss of €37.1 million, compared to €41.7 million in the same period last year.

Pharvaris’ strategic focus remains on advancing its pipeline, with significant milestones expected in the coming quarters. The company is well-funded, with a cash runway extending into the first half of 2027, supporting its ongoing and future clinical trials.

Looking ahead, Pharvaris is poised to continue its development efforts, with upcoming data readouts from key studies potentially reinforcing its position in the treatment of bradykinin-mediated diseases. The management remains committed to delivering innovative therapies that combine efficacy with the convenience of oral administration.

data.description:

Liberty Gold Corp., a company focused on developing open pit oxide deposits in the Great Basin of the United States, has released its third-quarter financial and operational results for 2025. The company is advancing the Black Pine Project in Idaho, a significant gold-producing region.

In the latest quarter, Liberty Gold reported several strategic developments, including the closure of a subscription agreement with Centerra Gold Inc., which acquired a 9.9% stake in the company. Additionally, the company received a staged payment for the sale of its interest in the TV Tower project and made key appointments to its corporate and project development teams.

Financially, Liberty Gold reported a loss of $6.25 million for the quarter, slightly higher than the previous year’s $5.6 million loss. However, the company’s cash and short-term investments increased significantly to $32.46 million from $6.97 million at the end of 2024, indicating improved liquidity. The Black Pine project continues to show promising metallurgical results, with consistent gold extractions and potential for resource expansion.

The Black Pine project has seen positive developments, with successful metallurgical testing and drilling results indicating the potential for economic reprocessing and resource expansion. The Rangefront Zone, in particular, has shown significant potential for future mining operations, with new exploration opportunities emerging.

Looking ahead, Liberty Gold remains focused on advancing its projects in the Great Basin, with continued exploration and development activities aimed at unlocking further value. The company’s management is optimistic about the potential for growth and profitability in the coming quarters.

Text S.A. Group of Companies, a Polish software company, specializes in software-related activities and operates in the technology sector. The company, which includes its subsidiary Text Inc. in the USA, is known for its innovative software solutions.

In its latest earnings report for the six months ending September 30, 2025, Text S.A. reported a net profit of PLN 59,652 thousand, a decrease from the previous year’s PLN 86,327 thousand. The company also noted a decline in net revenues from sales, which amounted to PLN 167,610 thousand compared to PLN 176,252 thousand in the same period last year.

Key financial metrics indicate a challenging period for Text S.A., with operating profit dropping to PLN 65,513 thousand from PLN 92,989 thousand a year earlier. The company’s total assets decreased to PLN 178,788 thousand from PLN 226,705 thousand as of March 2025. Despite these challenges, the company maintained its focus on product development and market adaptation, particularly in AI technologies.

The company’s management remains cautiously optimistic about the future, emphasizing the importance of adapting to rapid technological changes and leveraging AI to enhance operational efficiency. They continue to monitor the economic situation and exchange rate fluctuations closely, which are critical given the company’s significant USD-denominated revenues.

Seabridge Gold is a Canadian-based resource company primarily engaged in the acquisition and exploration of gold properties, with a focus on large-scale projects in North America.

In its latest earnings report for the quarter ended September 30, 2025, Seabridge Gold highlighted significant advancements in its KSM project, including ongoing field activities and partnership discussions, as well as the completion of a major drilling program at Iskut.

Key financial metrics revealed a net loss of $32.3 million for the quarter, compared to a $27.6 million loss in the same period last year. The company invested $52.9 million in mineral interests, a substantial increase from the previous year. Additionally, Seabridge’s net working capital improved to $83.2 million from $37.8 million at the end of 2024.

Strategically, Seabridge continues to focus on its KSM project, with progress in feasibility studies and infrastructure development, including the construction of a BC Hydro switching station. The company is also advancing partnership discussions with potential candidates to further the project’s development.

Looking ahead, Seabridge Gold remains optimistic about its strategic initiatives and the potential for partnerships to advance its projects. The company is preparing for a resource estimate at Iskut in early 2026 and anticipates a decision on its court case regarding the KSM project in the same period.

data.title: OPAP S.A. Reports Strong Financial Performance in 2025
data.description:
Greek Organization Of Football Prognostics ( (GOFPY) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Greek Organization Of Football Prognostics presented to its investors.

TipRanks Black Friday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for the data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

The Greek Organization Of Football Prognostics, commonly known as OPAP S.A., is a leading company in the gaming and lottery industry, primarily operating in Greece and Cyprus. It is renowned for its extensive portfolio of betting and gaming products, including lottery games, sports betting, and video lottery terminals (VLTs).

In its latest nine-month interim management statement for 2025, OPAP S.A. reported a solid financial performance, marked by a 6.5% increase in revenue compared to the same period in 2024. The company’s net gaming revenue (NGR) also saw a 6.3% rise, reflecting robust growth in both the online and retail sectors.

Key financial highlights include a profit before interest, tax, depreciation, and amortization (EBITDA) of €612.6 million, up 4.4% from the previous year. The company’s profit for the period reached €368.5 million, a 5.8% increase year-on-year. Notably, OPAP’s online sector recorded a 10.8% growth in gross gaming revenue (GGR), while its retail sector grew by 4.8%. The company also made strategic moves, such as acquiring the remaining 15.51% stake in STOIXIMAN LTD, further strengthening its position in the online sports betting and iGaming segments.

The company maintained strong cash flows from operating activities, with a net cash inflow of €521.4 million. However, cash outflows from investing activities increased due to higher acquisitions of intangible assets and property, plant, and equipment. Financing activities saw a significant cash outflow, primarily due to the acquisition of treasury shares and the purchase of a minority stake in STOIXIMAN LTD.

Looking ahead, OPAP S.A. remains focused on its growth strategy, emphasizing operational efficiency and strategic investments. The management is optimistic about the company’s future prospects, particularly in the expanding online gaming market, as it continues to leverage its strong market position and explore new opportunities.
